rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROY ELLIS KUTZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/27/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) What happened to the 5 shares of Kyndryl Holding, 13.596938 shares of Corteva, 12.719290 shares of The Chemoures Company, and 29.108560 shares of Dow? Are they written off because they had no value? Do the companies no longer exist? (2) If there are 20 shares of General Motors, 14 shares of Morgan Stanley Dean Witter, and 12 shares of Dean Witter, Discover & Co. on hand, each of the two heirs should receive only half the number of shares. (3) Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. (4) A petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.36 I.2.] (5) There was no cash in the estate. How were expenses of administration paid? Courtesy copies of an amendment to the petition, signed and verified by the administrator, must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/15/23. An amending pleading requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: (1) In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] (2) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] (3) In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.] (4) Letters of Administration, not Testamentary, were issued on 3/23/22, not when the administrator was appointed on 3/14/22. (5) A Summary of Account is unnecessary when there is no accounting. (6) When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YOUNG JOON CHO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/28/2023 per counsel's request. An appearance may be required on 08/28/2023 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 03/22/2023): 1) No proposed order has been received. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. The notice must be given to the petitioner's former attorney who requested special notice. Copies of the petition and supplemental papers must also be served. 3) The calculation of statutory compensation payable to the attorneys is incorrect. See CRC 7.705. 4) A copy of an agreement between the petitioner's former and current attorneys on the allocation of the statutory compensation must be filed. CRC 7.704. 5) Please provide a copy of a deed or other recorded instrument or a title report showing how the decedent held title to the real property at the time of his death. 6) The legal description of the real property including the APN must be provided. The proposed order must also include the legal description. CRC 7.651(b)(3). 7) If the real property is the only asset on hand, please provide a proof that sufficient cash has been advanced to the estate for payment of the statutory compensation and costs which must be ordered to be paid "out of" the estate. See Probate Code section 10831(d). If both attorneys consent to a lien on the real property with 5% simple annual interest per LRSF 14.56, the petition must be amended to request distribution of the real property subject to the lien. 8) No timely election was filed by the petitioner as the decedent's surviving spouse to probate her intestate share of separate property passing to her under section 13502(a)(1). If the petitioner is requesting a late election under section 13502(b), the statement required by LRSF 14.35(I)(5)("statement must clearly state that he/she has been fully informed about the reasons for a probate (rather than, e.g., a Spousal Property Petition) and the consequences thereof, including potential delay and increased fees") must be filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN PANARELLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/21/2023. The following issue remains unaddressed by Kemp & Associates: 1) No proposed order has been received. The following issue remains unaddressed by American Research Bureau and claimants who filed their statement of interest on 01/26/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received. The following issues remain unaddressed by the petitioner: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) The box at Item 1 of the DE-120 form must be unchecked, as no status report has been filed. 3) A copy of the remote appearance information attached to the DE-120 form must also include the CourtCall appearance information. 4) A copy of the decedent's death certificate shows 03/28/2021 as the date of death (not 03/20/2021). 5) As to the surviving issue of the decedent's maternal grandparents: i) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) to locate Charles Steven Madrid's contact information must be filed. ii) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) to locate Zachary Madrid's contact information must be filed. iii) If Benjamin Sandoval Jr. is incarcerated, notice under Probate Code section 9202(b) must be given. iv) No proof of heirship has been filed for Cordelia Womack. v) A certified copy of a spousal property order or an original small estate affidavit with a certified copy of David Lopez' death certificate must be filed to determine Mary Cooley's entitlement to distribution. vi) Frank Lopez Jr. appears to have survived the decedent. A certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative of his estate must be filed, or an original small estate affidavit with a certified copy of his death certificate must be filed to determine Terrance Lopez/Kevin Lopez' entitlement to distribution. vii) Please provide a copy of Denise Lopez' birth certificate or other proof of her child-parent relationship with Frank Lopez.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD GARLAND NOBLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been FILED to address the following issues: 1) 7% interest must be paid on the $3,000 cash gift to Catherine Noble, as required by Probate Code section 12003. The petition has not been amended to allege a specific amount of interest payable as of a date certain, together with a daily rate thereafter. 2) The Schwab account statement attached to the petition to show the assets on hand available for distribution does not list 770 shares of Bank of America common stock inventoried and appraised as assets that existed at the time of the decedent's death. No explanation has been provided as to what happened to those shares. The information must be provided despite the waiver of account. See CRC 7.550(b)(2) & (3). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LINDA L. TERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion #03 For Reconsideration/Terminating Of Anti-Human, Violent, Based On Fraud Data 4 Years Restraining Order; With Direct Open Address To Presiding Judge
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. On April 27, 2022, Judge Jospeh M. Quinn signed an Elder Abuse Restraining Order After Hearing in the Conservatorship of Linda L. Tern, PCN-21-304888. The Order restrains "Alexander Koustoph aka Alexander Kustov" from, among other things, having any contact with Ms. Tern or coming within 100 yards of her. By its terms, the Order expires on April 27, 2026. Mr. Koustoph has filed a request for "Reconsideration/Termination" of the April 27, 2022 Order, along with several "supplemental materials." Counsel for the Conservator has filed an Opposition. A hearing on Mr. Koustoph's request for "Reconsideration/Termination" of the April 27, 2022 Order is set for July 19, 2023 at 1:30 in Department 204 of the San Francisco Superior Court, 400 McAllister Street, San Francisco. The Parties are order to appear and present argument. Ms. Tern is not ordered to appear. Remote appearance is authorized. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUM CHUEN TO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/9/23. (No earlier availability.) 1. Nothing has been filed yet to explain under what authority Ka Yun To, the son of Wai Keung To, signed the waiver of bond on behalf of his deceased father. If he does not have any legal authority to have done so, the petitioner might wish to seek appointment with full bond. 2. When, in 2013, did Decedent's half-brother Shum Choen To die? Before or after Decedent died in 2013? 3. If Decedent's half-sister Yuk Ying To died in 2018, and half-sister Kwai King To died in 2021, they post-deceased. Who are their successors-in-interest? (Is anyone administering their estates or trusts or will their issue take pursuant to Probate Code Section 13100?) 4. Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MEN CHEW GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/9/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Now, another amended petition has been filed (e-filed on 7/6/23). It should have been captioned "SECOND AMENDED PETITION," etc., as there was already an amended petition filed on 4/20/23. (2) An amended pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] No proof of service or proof of publication of this second amended petition has been filed yet. (3) The caption of this [second] amended petition is incomplete if IAEA authority is being sought. (4) Item 6f of the petition does not appear to be the first box that applies if the decedent's brother and issue of a predeceased sister survived. (5) Item 8 of the amended petition is incomplete. a. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] b. Who were the decedent's parents and WHEN did they die? c. WHEN did the decedent's sister Ying Shui Seto die? d. Petitioner alleges that the decedent's parents "had only one other child, #5", but there was also the reportedly predeceased sister and four other niblings who do not appear to be the issue of either the decedent's surviving brother or of the predeceased sister. e. Sojen Gee is missing from item 8 of the [second] amended petition. (6) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] (7) Item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete (no telephone number). (8) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/2/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner other than the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Deadline is 9/20/23 for delivering the proposed order and endorsed filed courtesy copies of the petition and proof of service of the Notice of Hearing to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
